title: Cherry-Pick a Hotfix
description:
## Backport a Single Commit

A repository at **`/root/cprepo`** has a `main` branch and a `hotfix` branch. The `hotfix` branch contains one commit that adds **`fix.txt`** (with the content `hotfix-applied`); `main` does **not** have it. You are currently on `main`.

**Your task:** bring **only that commit** onto `main` using **cherry-pick** (do not merge the whole branch).

> 💡 `git cherry-pick <ref>` replays a single commit onto your current branch. The tip of `hotfix` is the commit you want.

hints.title: Cherry-pick the branch tip
hints.body: Cherry-pick applies the named commit onto the current branch. You can reference the hotfix branch tip by name.

setup_commands.command: cd /root/cprepo && echo base > base.txt && git add base.txt && git commit -m base
cd /root/cprepo && git switch -c hotfix && echo hotfix-applied > fix.txt && git add fix.txt && git commit -m 'add hotfix'

validation.description: Checks that fix.txt (with the hotfix content) is now present on main.

commands.description: main contains fix.txt with the hotfix content
